source_pr.title: tikv: make TestPanicInRecvLoop stable
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/16069 Problem Summary: ### What is changed and how it works? What's Changed: - make test can use a shorter dial timeout - make more debug info for deadline exceed error in batch client How it Works: ### Related changes - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch ### Check List Tests - Unit test Side effects - n/a ### Release note - No release note --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/pingcap/tidb/18900)

symptoms: `TestPanicInRecvLoop` intermittently hits deadline/connection establishment issues in the batch client under load, making the test unstable.
failure_signature: Deadline/connection timing instability in TiKV batch client during `TestPanicInRecvLoop` (e.g. context deadline exceeded while dialing/recv loop)
root_cause_explanation: `TestPanicInRecvLoop` depends on gRPC dialing and the TiKV batch client's send/recv loops. Under heavy load, connection establishment and deadline handling can be timing-sensitive, leading to intermittent `DeadlineExceeded`/dial-related failures. This PR stabilizes the test by making the RPC client's dial timeout configurable (so the test can use a shorter dial timeout) and by adding more annotated debug info for `DeadlineExceeded` errors in the batch client, improving determinism and debuggability.
fix_pattern: Make test timeouts deterministic: allow configuring a shorter dial timeout in `TestPanicInRecvLoop` and annotate `DeadlineExceeded` errors in batch send/recv loops for clearer failure signatures.

evidence: evidence: PR title: make TestPanicInRecvLoop stable.
evidence: adjusts RPC client timeout behavior and sets a shorter dialTimeout in the test to reduce flakiness (store/tikv/client_batch.go, store/tikv/client_fail_test.go).
